commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Pharao: the usual title of the kings of Egypt, in Ezechiel's time, Ezechiel xxxii. 2. Courtiers are often too ready to flatter the passions of the prince: these are punished along with Pharao (ver. 17); whence we may conclude, that they concurred with him, to take Sarai against her will.
